steps.title: Prove the component boundary
actions: Collect at least two stable uses or a clear primitive need.
Distinguish component logic from page composition.
Check the existing system before creating a new primitive.
exit_criteria: The boundary owns a coherent purpose and is not premature abstraction.

steps.title: Write the contract and state matrix
actions: Define purpose, anatomy, content limits, variants, states, events, semantics, focus, and extension points.
Mark impossible and conditional state cells explicitly.
Prefer composition or discriminated modes over flag combinations.
exit_criteria: Invalid combinations are impossible, rejected, or documented.

steps.title: Choose semantics and behavior
actions: Start from the native element or a proven accessible primitive.
Define keyboard and focus behavior from an established pattern.
Assign controlled and uncontrolled ownership only when needed.
exit_criteria: Name, role, value, state, focus, and keyboard behavior are unambiguous.

steps.title: Implement with tokens and resilient content
actions: Consume semantic tokens and allow content expansion.
Implement states and transitions from one source of truth.
Keep optional behavior out of the base payload.
exit_criteria: Representative and boundary fixtures render without local design-value drift.

steps.title: Test and document decisions
actions: Create deterministic state stories and interaction tests.
Test keyboard, assistive technology, widths, themes, and browsers by risk.
Document when to use, avoid, compose, and migrate the component.
exit_criteria: A new consumer can use the component correctly without author guidance.
